post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 203: Illuminati Mafia
Rules
  • Rules
    • 1.1: Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • 1.2: Do not post at night.
    • 1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • 1.4: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • 1.5: You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others. This does not include adding extra spaces.
    • 1.6: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • 1.7: If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • 1.8: Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • 1.9: Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#008000), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
    • 1.10: Do not quote moderator communications.
    • 1.11: Confirm by posting Confirm
  • Voting/Hanging
    • 2.1: Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. For example, "Vote: fdas" and Vote: fDAs is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", “vote: fdas, Vote:fdas, Vote: fads, Vote: fdsa, and "Vote: fdas" are not.
    • 2.2: Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • 2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • 2.4: You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Hang".
    • 2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • 2.6: Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
    • 2.7: If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • 2.8: If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • 2.9: If the deadline is met and no person is hanged, it will be considered no hang and will proceed to be twilight.
    • 2.10: The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • 2.11: During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • 2.12: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • 3.1: Each day will be 336 hours (2 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
    • 3.2: The deadlines will follow the official time
    • 3.3: The deadline will occur the very second 336 hours has occurred.
    • 3.4: The night deadline will be 72 hours after night begins.
    • 3.5: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • 3.6: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
    • 3.7: Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • 3.8: If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, you will receive an in-game prod.
    • 3.9: If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
    • 3.10: If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • 3.11: If a player receives 5 PM Prods, he/she will be replaced
    • 3.12: If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • 4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
    • 4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
    • 4.3: Follow all of the rules.
    • 4.4: Be mature.
    • 4.5: For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • 5.1: I fdas am the moderator of Mafia Game 203 and mathjay is the co mod.
    • 5.2: Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • 5.3: Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
    • 5.4: Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • 5.5: The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount
    • 5.6: If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • 5.7: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • 5.8: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

  • Read all of the rules

post_rendered:
Okay, so @mark888 seems to be an important topic at the moment so I'm going to talk about him/her here. I can see that my scumread on mark888 is something not agreed with by many. To be fair, I did misinterpret and overreact to post #1231 in my post #1232. Since post #1231 was the main reason I voted mark, Unvote. That said, (again this could be personal playstyle so my apologies if it is) I feel very uneasy about newbcards, partially because there is a Mafia FAQ a few tabs away from this one that answers most of mark's questions. As a result, mark is still a scumlean for me. If anything, I feel that my investigation of mark will at least counteract the near-universal townread on him, which could be dangerous if he starts to misdirect town.

Moving on—please just be careful with midway, he could always be fakeclaiming, longer explanation here.
[hide=Are you sure? This is very long...]So a lot of the time PR claimers are believed because it seems like they would be nked, and if they aren't nked, they're probably scum and thus would be hanged.
But midwaybear, an "Informed Townie", has essentially exhausted his PR purpose, and is no more than a VT at this point, thus making him at significantly lower risk of nks if he is town. As a result, he very well could not die soon if he is town, thus this "Informed Townie" role is a quite safe one to claim. I'm even thinking about {juliankuang, midwaybear} as w/w in a risky gambit that could actually work well.
Obviously, neither of them should be our D1 hangs, but still, I'm very wary of them especially in later days.
[/hide]

And now some responses to recent posts:
  • So @mark888's readlist actually seems towny to me; although the whole point system is a little weird (although that does make mark seem like newbtown, idk)
  • midwaybear#1256 wrote:
    I think the RT was some coded message, and apparently cw though julian was opportunistic. Julian is conf town, so I feel like this tells me that cw overreacts a lot and is hyper aggressive which makes sense. These tendencies generally come from town, but as I said earlier scum also has incentive to be aggressive because it makes them look good and they can target low hanging fruits. This quote is NAI for me, but it gives me something to expect.
    There was a bit of a misunderstanding with pronouns there—when I said "their", I meant Mathletesv, not juliankuang. I was saying that @juliankuang's ISO demonstrates that @Mathletesv was being opportunistic when responding to my RT. Sorry for the misunderstanding!
  • The fact that my RT (a.k.a. me hammering on the keyboard in the color yellow) is being suspected as scum code is literally unbelievable to me. Seriously, say there are around three scum in this game—that would mean that scum would be throwing out a code into the game that is actually more likely to be broken by town than scum XD. Again, if there is only nightchat, than scum wouldn't take the risk of throwing out a code when they wouldn't even have been able to share an encryption, and if there is daychat, they would just use that. Whatever, but still please use some logic.
  • MIDWAYBEAR STOP TELLING US JULIAN IS CONFTOWN. JULIAN IS ONLY CONFTOWN IF YOU ARE CONFTOWN, WHICH YOU HAVE GIVEN US NO PROOF OF (again, please see my post enclosed in hide tags above which demonstrates how scum could very easily fakeclaim this "Informed Townie" role and get away with it)
  • Response to kevinmathz—agreed that hotel (or his/her replacement) should be our D1 hang if nothing better comes up. I feel like your defense of mark is bordering on WIFOM since it's talking about how "scum wouldn't do this" (while then maybe scum would do it so you say that) but your WIFOM seems more anti-town than scummy, idk, since it seems well-intentioned.
  • But onto voting—I'm going to Vote: midwaybear because his insistence that he and julian must be conftown just doesn't sit well with me. I'm not saying he would be a good hang, or at least policy hang, but he ought to be pressured into justification of his claim and conftown statements.

Thank you very much!


Signed,
The Cow

asdf334 wrote:
i mean if there's another cop/rolcop you should probably cop hptl

And what do you think the odds of that are, first, and second, wouldn't that be lovely if both cops get outed. The fact that hptl is using a dubious claim to wriggle out of being the hang seems scummy—it's not like she's using reasonable logic, which is NAI. Seriously, when you think of Illuminati mafia, you might think of cult, masons, or this "Informed Townie" role, idk. BUT 1-SHOT COP?!?!?!?!

One other thing worth mentioning:

1-shot cop is actually a very good role for scum to claim. The reason is that often when people claim pr, town thinks "Ah well if they don't get nightkilled soon we'll know they're scum, so the claim can slide." A 1-shot cop, however, is not at significantly increased risk of nightkill if they survive N1 since they essentially become VT if they use their shot. As a result, 1-SC is a safe role for scum to claim. That combined with hptl's slips and the fact that 1-SC doesn't even make sense in this game make me feel that we should still hang hptl, while obviously waiting until somewhat closer to deadline.

Signed,
the cow

I think we should do this:

We hang the scummiest person
At night, I target whoever's next scummiest and cop, doc targets me,
I give the result the next day. If cop gets red, they claim and I get hung. If cop doesn't claim, you hang me and risk losing a vt.
If I'm town, town gets more information than if you had hung me immediately. If I'm scum, town still gets information from my result, and they don't risk hanging a pr.

The "slips" were trolling. I realized I would probably have to claim soon, so I gave up on acting towny.

Claiming 1-shot cop doesn't make me mafia. Take game 194, for example. apw claimed 1-shop cop, someone said the same thing, that "1-shot cop is a good claim for mafia, so anyone who claims 1-shot cop is mafia", apw was hung, and he flipped 1-shot cop.

You can hang me, but wait a day for me to use my cop.

post_canonical:
[quote=asdf334]btw we shouldnt hang until kevin comes back to explain his role[/quote]

I am a modified Professional Jobber that has a bulletproof vest.

I can use the normal four actions: Kill, Roleblock, Investigate, Protect. However, my probabilities are modified based on what alignment is hanged. Here are the modified probabilities. Let $p$ be $30$ if scum is hanged, $25$ if 3p is hanged and $20$ if town is hanged.

1 action used: $2p$% success rate, $100-2p$% fail rate
2 actions used: $2p$% success rate, $50-p$% fail rate, $50-p$% random rate
3 actions used: $p$% success rate, $50$% fail rate, $50-p$% random rate
4 actions used: $p$% success rate, $50-p$% fail rate, $50$% random rate

The probabilities are only applicable if I vote the hanged person. Otherwise, $p=25$.

On night 1 I used investigate on asdf and protect on HannahW. I got a guilty result. 

According to conditional probability, there are 11 alive players so a $\frac2{11}$ chance a random person is scum, assuming 1 mafia and 1 hostile 3p. Assuming everyone is equally likely scum, there is a $\frac{3}{4} \cdot \frac2{11} = \frac3{22}$ chance asdf is scum and I get scum (normal). If it's RNG'd there is a $\frac{1}{4} \cdot \frac2{11} = \frac1{22}$ chance I get scum (random), and in that random chance there is a $\frac{2}{11}$ chance that asdf is scum, too. Overall there is a $\frac{35}{242}$ chance asdf is scum and I get scum and a $\frac{9}{242}$ chance he is town and I get scum. $\frac{35}{35+9} \cdot 100 \approx 79.54\%$ chance asdf is scum. Just remember that.

Tonight I will roleblock asdf. What other actions should I do?

We should update the gamethrowing rules:

- Doing something that would definitely lower your chances of winning is considered gamethrow. For example, giving up when you think you'll be the hang is gamethrowing, and so is shooting a confirmed town as vigilante. However, it is not gamethrowing to, for example, hang confirmed town if they claimed oracle, because you get information, or fakeclaim a PR as town, because you can bait a kill.
